name: Post a daily Asana project status digest to Slack

parameters.content:
## Post a daily Asana project status digest to Slack

### How it works

1. A weekday-morning schedule starts the run in your timezone.
2. It reads one Asana project over the REST API, asking for due dates, assignees, and completion so every bucket has what it needs.
3. A Code node sorts open tasks into overdue, due today, due this week, and unassigned, collects tasks completed in the last day, and tallies open load per assignee.
4. A second Code node builds a Block Kit message and posts it to your Slack channel.

### Setup steps

- [ ] Add an Asana credential (Personal Access Token) and assign it to `Fetch Asana Project Tasks`.
- [ ] Add a Slack credential and assign it to `Post Digest to Slack`.
- [ ] In `Prepare Digest Config`, set `projectGid`, `slackChannel`, and `timezone`.
- [ ] Set the days and hour in `Every Weekday Morning`, run once to check, then activate.

### Customization

Change the schedule, the `lookbackHours` window for completed tasks, or how many task links each section shows. Every count is computed in n8n, so an optional model call can add a one-line summary without changing any number.

## Schedule and configure

Set `projectGid`, `slackChannel`, and `timezone` here, then choose which days and what hour the digest runs.

## Read the Asana project

The `opt_fields` list guarantees due dates, assignees, and completion come back for every task. `completed_since` keeps completed tasks scoped to the recent window.

## Bucket and count, no AI

Deterministic classification into overdue, due today, due this week, and unassigned, plus completed-in-the-last-day and per-assignee open load.

## Format and post the digest

Build the Block Kit message and post one digest to Slack. The final node keeps the built payload so each run is inspectable.

## Optional one-line summary

The counts come from the Code node, not a model. For a plain-English headline like '3 overdue, Priya carrying the most', add a Groq call that writes a `summaryLine` field and the digest shows it above the buckets. Off by default, and it never changes the numbers.
